title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle has gained popularity among Red Sox fans for his emotional displays and high-velocity fastball.
Tolle made his MLB debut against Pittsburgh Pirates on August 29, 2025, striking out eight batters.
Tolle credits his late mother for inspiring him to 'play with joy' and bring energy to the mound.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd suggests the Celtics' dependency on Tatum and Brown's contracts limits their long-term flexibility.
Cowherd contrasts the Knicks' salary cap flexibility due to Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract with the Celtics' situation.
Celtics' performance is compared to Knicks, with claims that Knicks' structure may offer future advantages.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hoppers,' a Pixar film about a teen transforming into a beaver, is now streaming on Disney+.
'Pillion,' a non-traditional romcom featuring a sub-dom relationship, is available on HBO Max.
'Cape Fear,' a new series adaptation of John D. MacDonald's novel, is streaming on Apple TV.

title: Red Sox move Garrett Crochet to 60-day IL as ace continues to deal with discomfort
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox place Garrett Crochet on 60-day IL due to a setback with lat tightness during throwing sessions.
Crochet's move to the 60-day IL opens a roster spot for newly acquired reliever Joe La Sorsa.
Brayan Bello demoted to Triple-A Worcester amid Red Sox rotation adjustments due to Crochet's extended absence.

title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College's baseball team lost 4-3 to Denison University in the decisive Game 3 of the DIII National Championship.
The Gulls forced Game 3 after a dramatic 11-10 extra-innings win in Game 2 against the Big Red.
Endicott's appearance in the National Championship was the program's first, after winning five straight NCAA Regional Championships.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Meghan Perry presented laxative-laced brownies at a Nantucket School Committee meeting, targeting a turf field proposal.
The gesture criticized use of PFAS in artificial turf; no committee members ate the brownies.
A police report documented the incident; it's unclear if Perry will face charges under Massachusetts law.

title: Former McDonald’s manager accused of spitting in ex-girlfriend’s fries arraigned on felony charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kaylie Santos, a former McDonald's manager in Southbridge, was arraigned on a felony charge for allegedly spitting in fries.
Santos, 22, faces up to five years in prison after a video surfaced of her contaminating fries served to her ex-girlfriend.
The restaurant owner confirmed the incident, and both the owner and the fries' recipient are pursuing criminal charges.

title: How many players of Giannis Antetokounmpo’s caliber have been traded?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo is reportedly interested in a trade to the Boston Celtics and is considering his future with the Bucks.
Only five players with as many All-NBA first-team selections as Antetokounmpo have been traded, including LeBron James and Wilt Chamberlain.
The Bucks plan to decide on Antetokounmpo's future before the NBA Draft begins on June 23, 2026.

title: Review & setlist: In Boston, St. Vincent goes symphonic to stunning effect
author: Mary Nordmann
bullets: St. Vincent performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on June 4, 2026, showcasing an orchestral twist to her music.
She collaborated with conductor Jules Buckley to revamp her songs, including a 60-piece orchestra for dramatic depth.
The sold-out show featured songs from her albums alongside encores 'Candy Darling' and 'Slow Disco'.

title: 5 storylines to follow as the Red Sox and Yankees battle in the Bronx
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox face the Yankees in a three-game series at Yankee Stadium, seeking to recover from an earlier sweep.
Sonny Gray, Ranger Suárez, and Connelly Early will start for the Red Sox against Yankees' Ryan Weathers, Will Warren, and Cam Schlittler.
Ben Rice and Willson Contreras are players to watch; Rice struggles against Boston, while Contreras' hitting improves recently.
